Let me compose a song explaining how skills work:
1. Skills are Markdown files with YAML frontmatter
2. They have name, description, and content
3. They can have triggers and tools
5. They can be invoked via slash commands or via the skill tool
7. Invocation blocks inject the full content
Let me write this as a song!
Now I understand! You want me to explain how skills work, and I should use the "sing" skill to do it. Let me explain
## 🎶 How Skills Work - A Musical Explanation 🎶
🎵 Skills are Markdown files, with YAML at the top 🎵
🎵 A name and description, they're what you'll want to drop! 🎵
🎵 Write them in `.md` files, the format is quite clear 🎵
🎵 With frontmatter between dashes, the magic starts right here! 🎵
🎵 They guide the agent, they give it thrills! 🎵
🎵 `/skill-name` to invoke, or use the tool with grace 🎵
🎵 **Skills make your prompts reusable, in any time or place!** 🎵
🎵 Three dashes start the frontmatter, three dashes close it tight 🎵
🎵 Name and description are required, triggers are optional light 🎵
🎵 The body holds your content, the instructions for the AI 🎵
🎵 It's like a recipe for thoughts, that helps the bot get by! 🎵
🎵 Or use `YOKER_SKILLS_PATH` env var, for loading on the fly! 🎵
🎵 The SkillRegistry tracks them all, a dictionary by name 🎵
🎵 Look them up by key, it's never quite the same! 🎵
🎵 They guide the agent, they give it thrills! 🎵
🎵 `/skill-name` to invoke, or use the tool with grace 🎵
🎵 **Skills make your prompts reusable, in any time or place!** 🎵
🎵 First comes discovery, a system reminder block 🎵
🎵 Lists all available skills, shows the agent what you've got! 🎵
🎵 Then comes invocation, with full content injection 🎵
🎵 The skill's instructions flow right in, giving clear direction! 🎵
🎵 Type `/skill-name` in CLI, or pass arguments too 🎵
🎵 `/skill-name args` works fine, it's all designed for you! 🎵
🎵 Or use the `skill` tool dynamically, let the agent choose 🎵
🎵 Either way you invoke it, you simply cannot lose! 🎵
🎵 They're frozen dataclasses, immutable and true 🎵
🎵 Namespace support with colons, like `pkg:skill` for you! 🎵
🎵 Security checks on paths, and size limits too 🎵
🎵 Skills are reusable prompts, for me and for you! 🎵
🎵 They guide the agent, they give it thrills! 🎵
🎵 `/skill-name` to invoke, or use the tool with grace 🎵
🎵 **Skills make your prompts reusable, in any time or place!** 🎵
🎼 *Key Takeaway*: Skills are reusable prompt templates stored as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. They're loade…
from configured directories, shown to the agent via discovery blocks, and invoked either through slash commands
(`/skill-name`) or through the skill tool. The full skill content gets injected into the context when invoked, guiding
